title: What Is an Enforcement Title and How to Obtain It?
description: We explain what an enforcement title is, how to obtain it, and what documents are needed to initiate bailiff enforcement.
content:
What Is an Enforcement Title?

An enforcement title is the fundamental document authorizing the commencement of bailiff enforcement proceedings. It consists of an enforcement order (tytuł egzekucyjny) bearing an enforcement clause issued by the court. Without this document, the bailiff cannot take any enforcement action against a debtor.

Types of Enforcement Orders

The following can serve as enforcement orders and, once an enforcement clause is appended, become enforcement titles:

  • Court rulings — judgments, payment orders, and court decisions awarding specific performance.
  • Court-approved settlements — settlements approved by the court during civil proceedings.
  • Notarial deeds — in which the debtor submitted to enforcement under Art. 777 of the Code of Civil Procedure.
  • Bank enforcement titles — although their use has been restricted, they still exist in legal transactions.
  • European enforcement orders — for uncontested claims in cross-border cases.

Enforcement Clause — What Is It and How to Obtain It?

The enforcement clause is a court order certifying that the given enforcement order is suitable for execution by enforcement proceedings. The application for granting an enforcement clause is filed with the court that issued the ruling — usually the district or regional court with jurisdiction over the debtor's place of residence or seat.

The original enforcement order or a certified copy must be attached to the application. The court considers the application at a closed session, without summoning the parties, generally within 3 days of filing.

Step by Step — From Judgment to Enforcement

  1. Obtain a final court ruling or another enforcement order.
  2. File an application for the enforcement clause at the competent court.
  3. Collect the enforcement title — the ruling bearing the clause.
  4. File an enforcement application with the court bailiff competent for the debtor's place of residence or seat.
  5. The bailiff initiates enforcement proceedings and takes steps to satisfy the creditor.

Electronic Writ-of-Payment Proceedings (EPU)

For monetary claims, the creditor may use a simplified route — the Electronic Writ-of-Payment Proceedings (e-court). The claim is filed online, and the payment order is issued without a hearing. Once granted an enforcement clause, the resulting order constitutes an enforcement title enabling the commencement of enforcement.

When Should You Contact the Bailiff?

You can file an enforcement application with the office immediately after receiving the enforcement title. There is no strict deadline for filing the application; however, the debtor's right to invoke the statute of limitations may hinder enforcement many years after the ruling was obtained.

Bailiff's Office No. IX in Otwock assists at every stage of proceedings — from explaining formal requirements to efficiently carrying out enforcement. We invite you to contact us.
author: Marcin Starzec
metaTitle: What Is an Enforcement Title and How to Obtain It? | Bailiff Otwock
